title: Add automated drift check between source strings and review return files
summary: Added Tools/localization-review-drift-check.py + Makefile target localization-review-drift-check + 10 unit tests under automation/tests/test_localization_review_drift_check.py + doc bullets in validation.md and localization-translation-quality.md. The tool compares each per-locale review return file's strings + stringsdict entries against the current source key sets and reports three drift dimensions: missing-in-return, stale-in-return, changed-english-value. Reporting-only by default; --check exits non-zero on drift; --json emits machine-readable output. Baseline as of 2026-05-18: 0 drift across all 18 non-English locales.

drift_dimensions: missing_strings_keys (source key added; not yet in return file)
stale_strings_keys (return file entry for key no longer in source)
changed_english_values (source copy edited after the review was recorded)

validation.make automation-check: passed (81 tests; 10 new for drift check)

not_claimed: the drift check is folded into make automation-check (it stays a separate Makefile target; CI/agents must invoke it explicitly)
the drift check validates translation quality (it only compares keys + english_value; flag-only, no quality assertion)
the drift check covers per-plural-row drift for stringsdict (it tracks stringsdict at the top-level key set granularity only; per-plural-category drift would need an extra pass)
residual_risk: Per-stringsdict-key plural-category drift (e.g., 'one' or 'other' added/removed) is not detected. The tool tracks top-level stringsdict keys only.
If a source english_value changes for a stringsdict entry's translated copy, it is not detected — only strings english_value comparisons are made.

The tool reads stringsdict via plutil (Apple-platform tool). On a non-macOS host, parse_stringsdict_keys returns empty set silently; consider adding a friendlier error if Owlory ever runs the tool outside macOS.
make localization-review-drift-check is not part of make automation-check. CI/agents must invoke it deliberately. Recommend adding to a release-preflight or future periodic check.
